memos.title: Housify: trust-first growth wedge
memos.summary: Position Housify as a trust-and-transaction platform instead of just a listing app. The strongest wedge is verified landlords, booking workflow, and M-Pesa-linked payments.
scoutIdeas: Launch a premium landlord verification tier with trust badges and faster tenant matching.
Create a relocation concierge upsell for busy professionals and diaspora clients.
Turn build progress into founder content to attract both users and investors.
criticResponse: A concierge model can distract from core workflow unless tightly scoped.
Verification only matters if users can clearly see why it reduces fraud and wasted time.
Investor content works best after the product flow is believable, not before.
finalRecommendations: Build the minimum trust layer first: verified landlord profiles, booking flow, and clear proof of legitimacy.
Test one monetization path immediately: landlord subscription or pay-per-verified-listing.
Capture before/after stories from the Nairobi rental process as content assets.
nextActions: Define the landlord verification workflow in product terms.
Draft a 30-day founder content sequence around trust in Nairobi housing.

memos.title: Portfolio-level recommendation: fewer fronts, stronger loops
memos.summary: The council's strongest cross-project recommendation is to concentrate effort on one flagship app, one cash-generating service, and one content engine rather than many parallel build tracks.
scoutIdeas: Use one flagship app as the credibility anchor.
Use services/consulting/concierge layers to create early cashflow around unfinished apps.
Use your build journey as content to compound reach and inbound opportunities.
criticResponse: Too many concept-stage projects create narrative fragmentation and execution drag.
Revenue experiments need short feedback loops, not abstract long-term plans.
A content engine without a focused flagship becomes noise.
finalRecommendations: Pick one flagship product to push toward investor-testable quality.
Pair it with one immediately monetizable service offer.
Use content as an amplification layer, not a separate burden.
revenueAngles: Concierge service around unfinished product workflows
nextActions: Choose the flagship project for the next 30 days.
Define one service offer that can monetize before full software completion.
Create a weekly council review cadence for new memos.
